title: LocalMind: OS-Integrated Local LLM Systems
summary: LocalMind is a systems-level framework that integrates large language models directly with core operating system abstractions, including file systems, memory management, and process-level interactions. The project explores how LLMs can act as intelligent system agents capable of controlled file access, stateful reasoning over local data, and task execution without cloud dependency. The focus is on secure, privacy-preserving, and deterministic interaction between LLMs and low-level system resources.

title: Side-Channel Attacks on Neural Networks
summary: This work investigates hardware-level information leakage in neural network systems, focusing on GPU-based side-channel vectors and peripheral interfaces such as HDMI and USB. The research demonstrates how indirect signals (e.g., timing, memory access, or I/O emissions) can be exploited to infer model behavior and sensitive inputs. The project includes experimental validation of attack pipelines and contributes toward understanding and mitigating side-channel risks in modern AI deployments.

title: VTON: Real-World Virtual Try-On Systems
summary: This project develops a practical virtual try-on system designed for real-world deployment under pose variation and occlusion. The approach leverages text-guided masking and diffusion-based generative modeling to produce realistic clothing synthesis while preserving body structure and garment semantics. The system is optimized for robustness and usability in applied settings such as e-commerce and interactive applications.

title: LLM-Based Evaluation for Software and Security Tasks
summary: This line of work studies the use of large language models as evaluators and agents for software engineering, coding, and security-related tasks. The project analyzes model performance across task categories such as code generation, vulnerability detection, reasoning, and system-level understanding. It includes benchmarking, prompt design, and structured evaluation pipelines to assess reliability and limitations of LLMs in technical domains.

title: IoT and Named Data Networking (NDN) Architectures
summary: This research focuses on scalable IoT architectures with an emphasis on Named Data Networking (NDN) and edge computing systems. The work includes simulation-driven analysis of large-scale network behavior, caching strategies, and data dissemination models. It also explores integration with learning-based approaches for optimizing task distribution and network efficiency in distributed environments.
